data.title: Replace Harold with python-control and Slycot
Establish the python-control and Slycot foundation
data.body:
## Goal
- Make python-control and Slycot the required model backend and remove Harold from the dependency contract.
- Define the control-native model and conversion boundary used by every identification algorithm.

## Context
- Keep SIPPY's `StateSpaceModel` wrapper and factory API.
- `StateSpaceModel.G` must become a `control.StateSpace`; `G_tf` and `H_tf` must be `control.TransferFunction` objects when the corresponding model exists.
- Use `control>=0.10.2` and `slycot>=0.7.0`; do not retain an optional-dependency or mock-model path for Harold.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Persistent tests are written before implementation changes and assert control-native object types, uppercase state matrices, sample-time preservation, and SISO/MIMO dimensions.
- `pyproject.toml`, `setup.py`, `uv.lock`, and the Docker image install control and Slycot without Harold.
- Shared transfer-to-state realization uses python-control with the Slycot method where needed and returns real NumPy matrices with stable shape conventions.
- `StateSpaceModel.G` is constructed with `control.ss(..., dt=ts)` whenever the model has inputs.

## Validation Gates
- `uv run pytest src/sippy/identification/tests/test_base.py`
- `uv run ruff check src/sippy/identification/base.py src/sippy/identification/tests/test_base.py`

data.title: Migrate direct polynomial identification algorithms
data.body:
## Goal
- Replace Harold construction and availability branches in ARX, FIR, OE, GEN, and BJ with python-control.

- Tests are updated first to assert behavioral transfer-function coefficients, delays, sample time, and state-space simulation rather than mocked Harold availability.

- Harold polynomial multiplication is replaced by NumPy operations with identical coefficient ordering.

## Validation Gates

data.title: Migrate SIPPY from python-control and Slycot to ctrlsys
Establish the ctrlsys-backed model boundary
data.body:
## Goal
- Replace SIPPY's direct python-control/Slycot model boundary with small SIPPY-owned state-space and transfer-function types backed by `ctrlsys` routines.
- Add `ctrlsys` as a required dependency while preserving sample time, SISO/MIMO shapes, and public model metadata.

## Context
- `ctrlsys` is a low-level C11/SLICOT binding rather than a high-level modeling package, so SIPPY must own the narrow model API it exposes.
- Use `tc04ad` for transfer-to-state conversion, `tb04ad` for state-to-transfer conversion, and `tb05ad` for state-space frequency response where their documented domains apply.
- Pass Fortran-order copies and reject every nonzero `info` result with a useful Python exception.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Persistent tests cover SISO/MIMO construction, conversion, timebase preservation, frequency response, and non-mutation of caller arrays.
- `StateSpaceModel.G`, `G_tf`, and `H_tf` use SIPPY-owned types instead of python-control objects.
- The new adapter contains the ctrlsys-specific array ordering and `info` handling in one place.
- The project dependency set includes `ctrlsys` and does not require callers to interact with raw SLICOT routine signatures.

## Validation Gates
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/uv run pytest src/sippy/identification/tests/test_base.py`
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/uv run ruff check src/sippy/`

data.title: Compact MIMO realization with ctrlsys
data.body:
## Goal
- Realize a complete MIMO polynomial matrix with tc04ad instead of independent channel blocks.
- Apply tb01pd minimal realization when it safely removes shared dynamics.

## Acceptance Criteria

data.title: Make predictor-form PARSIM-K the universal u-y estimator
data.body:
## Goal
- Implement the causal predictor-form PARSIM-K equations as the correctness path for both open- and closed-loop data.
- Reuse the current PARSIM implementation only where it matches the published causal and Toeplitz-structured formulation.

## Context
- Primary reference: Pannocchia and Calosi, `A predictor form PARSIMonious algorithm for closed-loop subspace identification`, DOI 10.1016/j.jprocont.2010.01.004.
- All non-causal future input/output terms must be removed row by row before Markov-parameter regression.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Estimated predictor Markov blocks match analytically generated `A_K`, `B_K`, `C`, `D`, and `K` blocks on noise-free fixtures.
- Plant matrices are consistently recovered from open-loop and feedback data without a loop-mode flag or measured controller.
- The final joint least-squares recovery of `B_K`, `D`, `K`, and `x0` is stable for MIMO and open-loop-unstable plants observed under stable feedback.
- Exactly deficient excitation produces a clear identifiability error; near-collinearity uses rank-revealing solves without silently inventing directions.
- Existing explicit `PARSIM-K` callers retain their API and sample-time/result contract.

## Validation Gates
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/uv run pytest src/sippy/identification/tests/test_parsim_k_reimplementation.py src/sippy/identification/tests/test_parsim_algorithms.py src/sippy/identification/tests/test_parsim_integration.py src/sippy/identification/tests/test_closed_loop_subspace.py`

data.title: Add automatic two-stage ORT-LQ projection for measured references
data.body:
## Goal
- Use measured exogenous excitation to separate deterministic input-output components before realization, without requiring callers to select a closed-loop algorithm.
- Implement both projection stages through compact LQ factors.

## Context
- Primary reference: Katayama and Tanaka, `An approach to closed-loop subspace identification by orthogonal decomposition`, DOI 10.1016/j.automatica.2007.02.011.
- A measured reference is an optional data capability, not a closed-loop declaration and not a requirement for consistency.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `IDData` can carry named exogenous reference channels; the canonical functional API can also accept an aligned reference array.
- The first LQ stage projects `[u; y]` Hankel rows onto `row(R)` without materializing a large projector; the second stage consumes only aligned deterministic components.
- Reference rank and independence diagnostics determine whether ORT is usable. An unusable optional reference falls back to the predictor estimator with a recorded reason and a focused warning.
- The implementation never substitutes measured plant input or past output as an exogenous instrument.
- With a valid reference and colored feedback disturbance, deterministic plant-response variance improves on the chosen regression fixture without changing the target plant.

## Validation Gates
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/uv run pytest src/sippy/identification/tests/test_iddata.py src/sippy/identification/tests/test_closed_loop_subspace.py src/sippy/identification/tests/test_subspace_lq_compression.py`

data.title: Add CVA realization weighting and automatic dimension selection
data.body:
## Goal
- Apply CVA-style whitening to the exact projected/predictor subspaces entering realization.
- Select practical horizons and model order when callers provide no subspace tuning options.

## Context
- CVA changes variance and conditioning; consistency must continue to come from the predictor equations or valid ORT projection.
- Reuse causal innovation likelihood and compact covariance factors already present in `subspace_core.py` where mathematically applicable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CVA weights use conditional/projected covariances, symmetric eigendecomposition, and retained-rank inverse square roots; they are never calculated from unrelated raw covariance matrices.
- Singular weighting falls back to the unweighted consistent subspace with structured metadata, not a different statistical target.
- Default horizon candidates respect sample count, channel dimensions, identified rank, and `f > n`; explicit expert overrides remain available.
- Default order selection combines rank/singular-value evidence with held-out causal innovation BIC and is deterministic for fixed data.
- CVA weighting is shown to reduce or preserve variance on a documented fixture and does not materially change the sample-count bias trend.

## Validation Gates

data.title: Document and benchmark loop-agnostic subspace identification
data.body:
## Goal
- Make the no-selection workflow the primary documented experience and record its computational cost.
- Complete repository-wide verification without publishing changes automatically.

## Acceptance Criteria
- README and user guide lead with `sippy.identify(y, u)` and explain that the canonical estimator is valid for open- and closed-loop records.
- Documentation distinguishes feedback-induced input-innovation correlation from MIMO input collinearity and explains optional measured-reference benefits.
- An example identifies the same plant from open- and closed-loop datasets without changing identification options.
- Benchmarks compare canonical SUBSPACE with current N4SID and PARSIM-K on SISO/MIMO records; the compact LQ path does not regress materially in time or memory.
- Full pytest, Ruff lint, Ruff format check, and diff checks pass; no task commits include incomplete `.ergo/` state.

## Validation Gates
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/uv run python Examples/example_new_architecture.py`
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/uv run ruff check src/`
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/uv run ruff format --check src/`
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/uv run pytest`
- `git diff --check`

Unresolved questions: none

## Scope
- Add one canonical subspace estimator that identifies the open-loop plant from ordinary `u,y` records whether those records were collected in open or closed loop.
- Build on SIPPY's current compact LQ and PARSIM work rather than creating a parallel identification stack.
- Automatically exploit measured exogenous reference signals through two-stage ORT/LQ and apply CVA weighting only after the bias-safe data equation is established.

## Non-goals
- Do not infer or expose an `open_loop`/`closed_loop` classifier.
- Do not require controller matrices or a measured reference for ordinary closed-loop consistency.
- Do not silently recover physically separate input channels when excitation is rank deficient.
- Do not change the mathematical meaning of explicit N4SID, MOESP, CVA, or PARSIM method names.

## Key decisions and assumptions
- "No user inputs" means no loop-mode or algorithm-selection input; callers still provide measured `u,y`, and may provide an aligned exogenous reference when it exists.
- The always-available correctness route is causal predictor-form PARSIM-K/PBSID-style estimation, which is applicable to both open- and closed-loop data.
- Two-stage ORT is an automatic variance/conditioning enhancement when independent, persistently exciting reference data is available.
- CVA is a realization metric, not the source of closed-loop consistency.
- Automatic selection chooses horizon, order, rank truncation, and weighting only within one `SUBSPACE` abstraction; advanced named algorithms remain opt-in for reproducibility.
- Validation targets the known plant using analytical Markov parameters, poles, FRF, and held-out open-loop behavior.

## Primary research
- Pannocchia and Calosi (2010), DOI 10.1016/j.jprocont.2010.01.004.
- Katayama and Tanaka (2007), DOI 10.1016/j.automatica.2007.02.011.
- van der Veen et al. (2013), DOI 10.1049/iet-cta.2012.0653.
- Chiuso and Picci (2004), DOI 10.1016/j.automatica.2004.05.008 and 10.1016/j.automatica.2004.05.009.

## Highest risks and mitigation
- Finite-sample order/horizon selection can be unstable: use causal held-out likelihood, deterministic candidate rules, and regression fixtures.
- ORT can destroy rank when reference excitation is weak: diagnose retained rank and fall back to the universal predictor route with explicit metadata.
- Changing the canonical default can expose hidden compatibility assumptions: preserve explicit method names and gate all public entry points before switching the default.

Unresolved questions: none

## Scope
- Add one canonical subspace estimator that identifies the open-loop plant from ordinary `u,y` records whether those records were collected in open or closed loop.
- Build on SIPPY's landed compact LQ, rank-robust CVA, causal order scoring, and reusable PARSIM regressions rather than creating a parallel identification stack.
- Automatically exploit measured exogenous reference signals through two-stage ORT/LQ and apply CVA weighting only after the bias-safe data equation is established.

## Landed foundations to reuse
- `852e239` provides `_LQProjection` and `_lq_compress_subspace_data` for compact N4SID/MOESP/CVA projections.
- `3e2c80c` provides `_ReusableRightLeastSquares` and compact PARSIM-P LQ regression machinery.
- `0d3eae7` and `1a15739` provide causal innovation information criteria and rank-robust CVA covariance whitening.
- `842b1bd` preserves named-method behavior for short or underdetermined records through an explicit compatibility fallback.
- `d3d677e` adds optional TC/DC FIR regularization. FIR is outside this epic; closed-loop work must preserve both LS-default and regularized FIR behavior through the full regression suite.

## Non-goals
- Do not infer or expose an `open_loop`/`closed_loop` classifier.
- Do not require controller matrices or a measured reference for ordinary closed-loop consistency.
- Do not silently recover physically separate input channels when excitation is rank deficient.
- Do not change the mathematical meaning of explicit N4SID, MOESP, CVA, or PARSIM method names.
- Do not replace or duplicate the landed compact numerical kernels.

## Key decisions and assumptions

- Validation targets the known plant using analytical Markov parameters, poles, FRF, and held-out open-loop behavior.

## Goal

## Context
- `1a15739` already provides retained-rank covariance inverse square roots; `0d3eae7` already provides causal innovation information criteria. Reuse both rather than adding parallel weighting or scoring code.

Use stable singular-value rank diagnostics

data.title: Implement SSARX closed-loop identification
data.body:
## Establish closed-loop benchmark baseline

Create a reproducible benchmark that measures held-out reconstruction, frequency-response error, pole error, stability, route selection, and runtime on published and synthetic closed-loop data.

Acceptance criteria:
- The benchmark can use the published MathWorks SSARX example data and OpenMCT motor data, while tests remain deterministic and network-independent.
- Persistent tests exercise the benchmark and enforce reconstruction-quality gates.
- Documentation clearly separates dataset provenance from algorithms implemented by SIPPY.
- Ruff and the focused benchmark tests pass.

Validation:
- `uv run ruff check benchmarks/benchmark_closed_loop.py src/sippy/identification/tests/test_closed_loop_benchmark.py`
- `uv run pytest src/sippy/identification/tests/test_closed_loop_benchmark.py`

## spike: derive SSARX implementation contract

Study primary SSARX, VARX/PBSID literature and public reference behavior. Map the high-order VARX predictor, structured projection/subspace extraction, and state-space recovery stages onto SIPPY's existing subspace infrastructure. Record the equations, conventions, public options, failure semantics, and validation oracle directly in the dependent implementation task before completing this spike.

Acceptance criteria:
- Primary sources establish the intended SSARX method and distinguish it from CVA/N4SID and generic predictor fallbacks.
- Sign, lag, direct-feedthrough, state-order, and horizon conventions are explicit.
- The implementation task contains sufficient mathematical and API detail to proceed without relying on memory of the research.

## Implement SSARX estimator and public registration

Implement SSARX as a first-class identification algorithm using shared SIPPY data, numerical, and state-space infrastructure. Add or extract focused shared helpers where they preserve current behavior and reduce duplicated predictor/realization logic.

Acceptance criteria:
- `SSARX` is registered through the factory and follows the standard `(y, u)` or `IDData` contract.
- Sample time, SISO/MIMO shapes, deterministic options, model order, and diagnostics are preserved in the returned model.
- The implementation performs a high-order VARX/predictor estimation and SSARX subspace realization rather than aliasing an existing method.
- Rank deficiency and invalid horizons fail with actionable errors or documented fallbacks.
- Persistent unit and API tests cover the new route.

Validation:
- `uv run ruff check src/`
- `uv run pytest src/sippy/identification/tests/ -k ssarx`

## Prove SSARX reconstruction and robustness

Add behavioral and numerical validation for reconstruction quality under representative closed-loop conditions. Compare coordinate-invariant input-output behavior and dynamics rather than raw state matrices.

Acceptance criteria:
- Tests cover SISO and MIMO systems, colored noise, correlated feedback inputs, low and high SNR, and feedback-stabilized unstable dynamics where supported.
- Tests exercise direct feedthrough and delays when applicable to the implementation contract.
- Held-out simulation, Markov/frequency response, pole, and stability checks use an independent model/data oracle.
- Regression tests distinguish genuine SSARX success from fallback route success.

Validation:
- `uv run pytest src/sippy/identification/tests/ -k 'ssarx or closed_loop'`

## Benchmark SSARX against current closed-loop routes

Extend the closed-loop benchmark to execute SSARX directly alongside SIPPY's current predictor and orthogonal-projection routes, and document reconstruction accuracy and runtime.

Acceptance criteria:
- Published and synthetic datasets invoke the registered SSARX estimator by name.
- Output includes route, order, stability, held-out NRMSE, frequency-response error, pole error, and timing.
- Persistent gates catch reconstruction or material runtime regressions without network dependence.
- README results and limitations match measured output.

Validation:
- `uv run python benchmarks/benchmark_closed_loop.py --skip-motor`
- `uv run pytest src/sippy/identification/tests/test_closed_loop_benchmark.py`

## Validate and publish SSARX to main

Run the complete project validation, close the Ergo plan with evidence, commit plan state, push `main`, and verify local, tracking, and remote references agree.

Acceptance criteria:
- Ruff check and format verification pass.
- The full test suite passes without new skips or xfails masking SSARX failures.
- All implementation, tests, benchmark, documentation, and final Ergo state are committed atomically by milestone.
- Local `main`, `origin/main`, and the live remote SHA match after push.

Validation:
- `uv run ruff check src/ benchmarks/`
- `uv run ruff format --check src/ benchmarks/`
- `uv run pytest`

- The implementation performs a high-order VARX/predictor estimation and SSARX subspace realization rather than aliasing an existing method.
- Rank deficiency and invalid horizons fail with actionable errors or documented fallbacks.
- Persistent unit and API tests cover the new route.

Validation:
- `uv run ruff check src/`
- `uv run pytest src/sippy/identification/tests/ -k ssarx`

data.title: Prove SSARX reconstruction and robustness
data.body:
Add behavioral and numerical validation for reconstruction quality under representative closed-loop conditions. Compare coordinate-invariant input-output behavior and dynamics rather than raw state matrices.

Acceptance criteria:
- Tests cover SISO and MIMO systems, colored noise, correlated feedback inputs, low and high SNR, and feedback-stabilized unstable dynamics where supported.
- Tests exercise direct feedthrough and delays when applicable to the implementation contract.
- Held-out simulation, Markov/frequency response, pole, and stability checks use an independent model/data oracle.
- Regression tests distinguish genuine SSARX success from fallback route success.

Validation:
- `uv run pytest src/sippy/identification/tests/ -k 'ssarx or closed_loop'`

data.title: Benchmark SSARX against current closed-loop routes
data.body:
Extend the closed-loop benchmark to execute SSARX directly alongside SIPPY's current predictor and orthogonal-projection routes, and document reconstruction accuracy and runtime.

Acceptance criteria:
- Published and synthetic datasets invoke the registered SSARX estimator by name.
- Output includes route, order, stability, held-out NRMSE, frequency-response error, pole error, and timing.
- Persistent gates catch reconstruction or material runtime regressions without network dependence.
- README results and limitations match measured output.

Validation:

- `uv run pytest src/sippy/identification/tests/test_closed_loop_benchmark.py`

data.title: Validate and publish SSARX to main
data.body:
Run the complete project validation, close the Ergo plan with evidence, commit plan state, push `main`, and verify local, tracking, and remote references agree.

Acceptance criteria:
- Ruff check and format verification pass.
- The full test suite passes without new skips or xfails masking SSARX failures.
- All implementation, tests, benchmark, documentation, and final Ergo state are committed atomically by milestone.
- Local `main`, `origin/main`, and the live remote SHA match after push.

data.title: Implement SSARX estimator and public registration

data.body:
Implement Jansson's 2003 SSARX estimator as a first-class algorithm using shared SIPPY data, numerical, and result infrastructure.

Mathematical contract:
- Use the innovations model `x[k+1] = A x[k] + B u[k] + K e[k]`, `y[k] = C x[k] + D u[k] + e[k]` and predictor matrices `A_tilde = A - K C`, `B_tilde = B - K D`.
- Fit one high-order VARX regression `y[k] = D u[k] + sum(j=1..p) (C A_tilde^(j-1) B_tilde) u[k-j] + sum(j=1..p) (C A_tilde^(j-1) K) y[k-j] + e[k]`. Default `D=0`; allow explicit direct feedthrough only when the experiment has no instantaneous feedback algebraic loop.
- Build the lower block-Toeplitz future-input matrix `Phi_tilde` with diagonal `D` and subdiagonals `C A_tilde^j B_tilde`, and the strictly lower block-Toeplitz future-output matrix `Psi_tilde` with subdiagonals `C A_tilde^j K`.
- Form `z_f = y_f - Phi_tilde u_f - Psi_tilde y_f`. Estimate the state map by Jansson's CCA: SVD of `Rzz^(-1/2) Rzp Rpp^(-1/2)`, retain the requested/detected order, and set `X = V_n^T Rpp^(-1/2) p`.
- Estimate `[C,D]` from `y[k]` on `[X[k],u[k]]`, obtain innovations from that residual, then estimate `[A,B,K]` from `X[k+1]` on `[X[k],u[k],e[k]]`.
- Undo channel scaling consistently: `B` and `D` by input scales, `C` and residual/innovation channels by output scales, and `K` by inverse output scales. Preserve the state basis.

API and failure contract:
- Register method `SSARX`; support standard `(y,u)` or `IDData`, preserve sample time, and return `StateSpaceModel` with `K`, `x0`, variance, singular values, rank diagnostics, selected order, future horizon, and VARX order in `identification_info`.
- Options: `ss_f` future horizon, `ss_p` VARX/past order (default at least `ss_f`), `ss_fixed_order`, `ss_threshold`, `ss_d_required`.
- Require positive integer horizons, `ss_f > order` for a fixed order, enough usable columns, and full numerical row rank for the VARX and CCA past regressors. Raise actionable `ValueError` messages wrapped by the algorithm context; do not silently alias or fall back to PARSIM/CVA.
- Use existing `prepare_subspace_data`, factory/result contracts, and focused right least-squares/rank helpers where their conventions match. Extract a genuinely shared helper only if existing algorithms preserve behavior.

Validation oracle:
- At the core level, simulate a known innovations model and compare fitted predictor Markov blocks against independently computed `D`, `C A_tilde^j B_tilde`, and `C A_tilde^j K`.
- At model level, compare coordinate-invariant held-out simulation, Markov parameters/frequency response, poles, stability, and explicit route metadata against known SISO/MIMO plants; never compare raw state matrices.
- Include the Jansson closed-loop example (`u=r-y`, D constrained to zero), colored/correlated noise, high and low SNR, feedback-stabilized unstable dynamics, IDData sample time, and direct-feedthrough coverage under a well-posed experiment.

Acceptance criteria:
- `SSARX` is registered through the factory and follows the standard data contract.
- The code visibly implements high-order VARX, structured SSARX residualization/CCA, and state-space recovery rather than invoking an existing algorithm.
- Persistent unit/API/behavioral tests cover dimensions, accuracy, and failure semantics.

Validation:
- `uv run ruff check src/`
- `uv run pytest src/sippy/identification/tests/ -k ssarx`
